title: Hive Digital enhances its Bitcoin accumulation strategy to avoid stock dilution and debt
text: Hive Digital is strengthening its Bitcoin reserve strategy to reduce dependence on debt and equity dilution. The company leverages recent market downturns to expand mining operations and acquire new facilities, reflecting a broader industry trend toward cryptocurrency conservation rather than immediate sales. According to CFO Darcy Daubaras, the company maintains a long-term hold strategy for Bitcoin. This approach provides better liquidity management during bear markets, particularly following Bitcoin's recent 30% decline. Daubaras noted that retaining mined BTC is preferable to heavy use of debt or the dilution of shares to finance operations, practices increasingly common in mining. Many public miners have escalated stock dilution due to rising interest rates and reduced credit availability, forcing rapid Bitcoin sales to cover operational costs. However, Hive selectively sells Bitcoin for strategic investments—notably financing a 200-megawatt Bitfarms facility in Paraguay. By year-end 2024, Hive had expanded its Bitcoin reserves to 2,805 BTC. To address volatility, competition, and rising energy costs, Hive diversified its business model. In September 2023, it repurposed Nvidia GPUs for artificial intelligence tasks, generating over $2 per GPU-hour compared to $0.12 from cryptocurrency mining.
